body.title: UC internal restart endpoint — one call instead of kill + relaunch
body.result: Already DISCARDed per existing blocked reason: UC retiring as part of ADC Phase 5. Cutover ticket will be filed with Phase 5 batch. Closing administrative leftover.
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Right now restarting UC to pick up code changes requires `kill $(cat ~/.TheIgors/utility_closet.pid)` + relaunch. That risks dropping web browser, CC's MCP channel, and Igor's uc_client registration on the floor with no coordination. Needed: a single call (HTTP endpoint or rack operation) that does graceful shutdown + re-exec, so every attached client has a well-defined reconnect point.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/utility_closet_server.py (new endpoint + re-exec logic), wild_igor/igor/web/utility_closet_client.py (reconnect-on-disconnect detection), lab/claudecode/cc_skills/readigor/SKILL.md (maybe — if restart becomes a readigor subcommand)
**Design rules:** docs-live-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** IN — (1) POST /api/platform/restart triggers graceful shutdown + os.execv re-exec; (2) Igor's uc_client detects 'UC unreachable' and re-registers on first successful /health after; (3) minimum wait-for-healthy loop so caller knows when the new process is serving. OUT — CC's MCP-tool reconnection (separate concern — MCP client-side logic is outside UC's scope); browser-side reconnect (already handled by frontend JS reconnect-on-close).
**Test plan:** (a) unit test the new endpoint returns 202 + a reconnect-token before shutdown; (b) unit test Igor's uc_client re-registration path (mock UC returning /health before and after); (c) integration-ish: launch UC in a subprocess, hit /api/platform/restart, assert new PID and health=ok within 5s.

Architectural context: eventually both CC and Igor are racks in the utility closet (per Akien 2026-04-23). A UC that can cleanly restart itself is the foundation for rack-lifecycle operations — add/remove/upgrade/restart any rack without the whole platform going dark. This ticket is the first step toward that.
